result: OPS-LOG DISRUPTED ARE A DIFFERENT POPULATION — MODEL FAILS

expert_disrupted.description: High-performance H-mode shots, specific disruption types

ops_disrupted.description: Diverse: VDE from FA cutout, locked modes, early termination, density limits, commissioning issues

benchmark_all_2584.note: Model trained on expert-type disruptions cannot detect ops-type disruptions

benchmark_24000plus.note: Still fails even restricting to same shot range

root_cause: 1. Expert disrupted (disruption-py) are a CURATED subset of specific disruption types
2. Ops disrupted include EVERYTHING operators called a disruption (FA trips, early VDEs, gas issues, etc.)
3. Our onset labeling (rate anomaly detection) doesn't work for diverse disruption types
4. The 83 expert disrupted are NOT representative of ALL MAST disruptions
5. This is a LABEL QUALITY problem, not a model problem
implication: Need disruption TIME from ops log (e.g. 'disrupted at 350ms') to create proper labels, not just disrupted/clean binary
next_step: Parse disruption TIME from ops comments → proper onset labels → retrain
